ActionsTermsTC / SCPublicationsEditionsHarmoniseID ConflictsDivergence
Registry / Terms / influence quantity

influence quantity

VIMOutdated — VIM 1993 G 18 #00374 · 55 instances

Authoritative definition

VIM 1993 · concept #2.7view ↗

quantity that is not the measurand but that affects the result of the measurement

Outdated: Cites VIM 1993. Latest: VIM 2012.
  • VIM 1993 · #2.7
    quantity that is not the measurand but that affects the result of the measurement

Suggested actions

  1. INFO ✓ This term IS in VIM 2012 (concept #2.52). View VIM 2012 entry
  2. MEDIUM 42 distinct definitions — harmonise.

Publication instances

42 distinct definitions across 55 publications. 10 definitions are shared by multiple publications (merge candidates).

3 identical2010202X
quantity that is not the subject of the measurement but which influences the values of the measurand or the indication of the instrument

quantity that, in a direct measurement, does not affect the quantity that is actually measured, but affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result [VIM 2.52]

quantity that is not the measurand but that affects the result of the measurement [VIM: 1993, 2.10]

20102000OIML R 60:20002.5.100745
quantity that is not the measurand but that affects the result of the measurement. [VIM:1993, 2.7] (For example, temperature or humidity level at the instant the measurements on the load cell are being observed or recorded.)
pending

20102008OIML R 99-1:20083.2402357
quantity that, in a direct measurement, does not affect the quantity that is actually measured, but affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result

202X2022OIML R139-1:20223.4.103123

pending
202X2024OIML R049-1:20243.4.102421
quantity that, in a direct measurement, does not affect the quantity that is actually measured, but affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result [SOURCE: ISO/IEC Guide 99:2007/OIML V2-200:2012 (VIM) 2.52 [1],.modified – original exampes and notes removed; “EXAMPLE” added] EXAMPLE:The ambient temperature of the meter is an influence quantity, whereas the temperature of the water passing through the meter affects the measurand.
pending
202X2013OIML D011:20133.1502229-D011
quantity that, in a direct measurement, does not affect the quantity that is actually measured, but affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result [VIM 2.52][VIML 0.07
pending
